$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `group_character_members` repo — the
FIRST mount-index sibling-DB repo of Phase 2. One committed file drives BOTH
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness group_character_members tier-2 test).
SIBLING DB: in v4 this table lives in quilltap-mount-index.db (NOT the main
DB). The builder/oracle point SQLITE_MOUNT_INDEX_PATH at the fixture and read
it back through getRawMountIndexDatabase(); the Rust Writer simply opens that
file by path. This corpus banks the MACHINERY (sibling-DB round-trip), not a
new marshaling shape — the table is the plainest join: id + two required
UUID-as-TEXT columns (groupId, characterId, both cross-DB refs stored as
plain TEXT, no FK constraint) + the two timestamps.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one every other
tier-2 fixture uses. It is NOT the real Friday pepper and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.

ops.comment: Add a third character to the same group.
Re-point seed row 1 to a different character. createdAt preserved, updatedAt pinned.
Remove the second-group membership created above.
